title: Re-queue blocked parent tester/reviewer when corrective completes
description: Currently when a corrective developer bead completes, the scheduler creates new auto-followup test/docs/review children for it, but the original blocked tester or review bead that triggered the corrective stays blocked permanently — requiring manual retry. Fix: after a corrective developer bead completes (identified by the corrective_suffix in its bead_id), look up its parent bead. If the parent is blocked and is a tester or review agent type, reset it to ready so it re-runs and validates the fix. This avoids both the orphaned blocked bead problem and the creation of a redundant new test bead for the corrective. The re-queue should happen in the scheduler's _finalize() or corrective completion path in src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py. Also suppress auto-followup creation for corrective beads entirely — a corrective's job is to fix something, not to spawn its own test pipeline.
acceptance_criteria: When a corrective developer bead completes, its blocked parent tester or review bead is reset to ready
No new auto-followup test/docs/review beads are created for corrective beads
Non-corrective developer beads are unaffected — legacy auto-followup behavior preserved

handoff_summary.completed: Implemented scheduler changes in src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py: added shared corrective-bead detection, requeue-on-corrective-complete for blocked tester/review parents, and suppressed auto-followup creation for corrective developers by metadata or corrective suffix. Verified with `uv run python -m py_compile src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py` and `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"`.
handoff_summary.remaining: Tester agent should run the repository test suite to confirm the behavioral acceptance criteria, since test execution is out of scope for the developer agent.
handoff_summary.risks: Behavior relies on corrective bead identification via either `metadata.auto_corrective_for` or the configured corrective suffix appearing in the bead id. Full regression coverage was not run here because test execution is reserved for the tester agent.
handoff_summary.next_action: Run the scheduler/tester validation flow to confirm blocked tester/review beads are requeued after corrective completion and that corrective developer beads do not create new auto-followup test/docs/review children.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Low. Only src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py changed; downstream agents touching the same scheduler completion/followup paths should rebase carefully around the new `_is_corrective_bead` and `_requeue_parent_after_corrective_completion` logic.

conflict_risks: Low. Only src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py changed; downstream agents touching the same scheduler completion/followup paths should rebase carefully around the new `_is_corrective_bead` and `_requeue_parent_after_corrective_completion` logic.

Updated scheduler corrective completion handling so completed corrective developer beads immediately requeue their blocked tester/review parent, and hardened corrective detection so corrective developers never spawn auto-followup pipeline beads.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
